design_philosophy.core_loop: Progress through genre-shifting stages (platforming, overhead driving, first-person shooting) while uncovering a conspiracy involving an alien drug called 'Gel' and the B.E.D.A. Corporation
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Genre variety and environmental hazards in platforming segments; enemy vehicles and road navigation in driving segments; target tracking and ammunition management in shooting segments
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Secret agent detective investigating a shadowy corporation; multi-skilled operative capable of combat, driving, and marksmanship
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Ambitious multi-genre design similar to The Adventures of Bayou Billy—refuses to stay in one mechanical space. Heavily narrative-focused with anime/noir/biopunk aesthetic including substantial cutscenes for 1991. Uncompromising tone with violence and drug themes (censored in US release). Protagonist is a clone designed to replace himself, inverting typical hero narrative.

difficulty.lives_system: Limited lives system with health gauge per stage
difficulty.checkpointing: Stage-based progression with movie-style narrative sequences between stages
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Player can collect coins (100 = 1 extra life) and health restoration items; weapon switching (Laser Whip, .44 Magnum, M-24 Grenades) allows tactical adaptation. Speedrun community records show ~13 minute completion times, suggesting tight, learnable design.

progression.upgrade_system: Weapon switching between Laser Whip (infinite, short-range), .44 Magnum (limited ammo), and M-24 Grenades (limited ammo); ammo replenished by enemy drops. Health restoration items and coin collection for extra lives.
progression.unlock_mechanism: 11 stages unlock sequentially: Route 246 (driving) → Waterfront/Construction Site → Chinatown → Port → Ricardo Range → Warehouse 0001 → Train/Sewers → Power Plant → Bio-Tech Laboratory → Harbor (driving) → BEDA Corporate Headquarters (final)
replayability_hooks: Hidden Stage Select accessible via controller reset + A button (second controller)
Speedrunning community active (69 recorded runs, 29 players)

world_building_techniques: Large number of movie-style cutscenes between stages
Environmental storytelling through stage progression (investigation path: street → underworld → jungle → industrial sites → lab → HQ)
Character dialogue and relationships (partner Reese, lover Christy, acquaintance Sophia)
Thematic visual storytelling (cloning vats, human experimentation, cyborg transformation)

boss_design.philosophy: Multi-form confrontations serving narrative climax. Bosses represent both literal obstacles and plot revelations—early enemies are mundane/technological, later bosses are transformed humans and alien-influenced beings, culminating in fighting an aged clone of the protagonist
notable_bosses: Stage 1 boss: High-tech truck driver (revealed to be a monster; substance discovery catalyst)
Stage 3 boss: Sorcerer Kim Long/Kim Ron (mafia connection)
Stage 6 boss: Flying cyborg (revealed as transformed partner Reese; shoots Hart)
Stage 9 boss: Slimy mutant (revealed as transformed lover Christy; dies after battle)
Stage 11 boss: Aged clone director (human form → monster form; final confrontation)

danger_signals: Disappearance of partner Reese during previous case
Drug 'Gel' with addictive properties and severe side-effects
Kidnapping of Christy as bait/experiment subject
environmental_storytelling: Ricardo Range jungle: Remote military research base
Bio-Tech Laboratory: Human experimentation center with cloning vats
BEDA Corporate Headquarters: Final reveal of corporate conspiracy
